Title: Molecular mechanisms of high levels of L-ascorbic acid accumulation in chestnut rose fruits

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; GL3-like, MBW complex component
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during flower/fruit development; relatively higher correlation with AsA accumulation in bud stages than RroxEGL3, but not significantly correlated overall with AsA across development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis leaves increased AsA and trichome number
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox buffer in plants; beneficial nutrient for human health
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit in chestnut rose; leaves in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes AsA accumulation indirectly and increases trichome formation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RroxGL3 binds the RroxTTG2 promoter and activates its expression. 2. Overexpression in Arabidopsis increased leaf AsA by 54%–82% and strongly increased trichome number. 3. RroxGL3 complemented the Arabidopsis gl3 mutant for both trichome and AsA defects.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC/yeast one-hybrid for promoter activation of RroxTTG2; heterologous overexpression and complementation in Arabidopsis; metabolite and trichome phenotyping
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary regulator for engineering AsA and epidermal traits, but pleiotropic developmental effects should be considered
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increases trichome number and anthocyanin accumulation, indicating pleiotropy
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/annotation: NGDC GWHETKS00000000.1, PRJCA027126; RNA-seq: PRJCA027090
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although mechanistically important upstream of RroxTTG2, the paper emphasizes RroxTTG2 rather than RroxGL3 as the decisive regulator of high AsA accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Rosa roxburghii; Lactuca sativa; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Guinong 5; Italian lettuce (L. sativa L. var. ramosa Hort.); Col-0
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: D-galacturonic acid pathway of L-ascorbic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes methyl ester groups from pectin, facilitating pectin breakdown and supply of D-galacturonic acid precursor for AsA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox buffer in plants; beneficial nutrient for human health
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit in chestnut rose; leaves in transgenic lettuce and Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during fruit development; positively correlated with AsA accumulation in R. roxburghii
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in R. roxburghii fruit under 25°C low-temperature/optimal accumulation condition relative to hotter conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RroxPME expression was significantly positively correlated with AsA accumulation in developing R. roxburghii fruit. 2. Overexpression in lettuce increased leaf AsA by about 1.0- to 1.7-fold over WT. 3. RroxTTG2 binds the RroxPME promoter and activates its expression, linking transcriptional regulation to high AsA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and qPCR correlation analysis; heterologous overexpression in lettuce and Arabidopsis; dual-LUC; yeast one-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development stage; temperature differences among growing regions; 25°C treatment enhanced expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) for single-gene overexpression; native promoter in one multigene design
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; important upstream contributor in D-galacturonic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful component of multigene engineering to elevate AsA, especially combined with RroxGalUR, RroxDHAR2, and RroxTTG2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/annotation: NGDC GWHETKS00000000.1, PRJCA027126; RNA-seq: PRJCA027090
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text calls PME a key D-galacturonic acid pathway gene contributing to exceptionally high fruit AsA in chestnut rose.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Rosa roxburghii; Lactuca sativa; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Guinong 5; Italian lettuce (L. sativa L. var. ramosa Hort.); Col-0
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: D-galacturonic acid pathway of L-ascorbic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces D-galacturonic acid to L-galactonic acid in the D-galacturonic acid pathway toward AsA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox buffer in plants; beneficial nutrient for human health
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit in chestnut rose; leaves in transgenic lettuce and Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during fruit development and strongly positively correlated with AsA accumulation in R. roxburghii
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in R. roxburghii fruit under 25°C condition; expression stronger in high-AsA R. roxburghii than low-AsA R. multiflora
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes AsA accumulation and may play a major role among tested enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RroxGalUR expression was highly positively correlated with AsA accumulation in developing R. roxburghii fruit. 2. Overexpression in lettuce increased leaf AsA by about 1.2- to 2.0-fold, and overexpression in Arabidopsis also increased AsA. 3. VIGS of RroxGalUR reduced AsA concentration. 4. A unique 545-bp promoter insertion containing a W-box and LTR element is specific to R. roxburghii and underlies stronger activation. 5. RroxTTG2 binds the inserted W-box and activates RroxGalUR expression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and qPCR correlation analysis; heterologous overexpression in lettuce and Arabidopsis; VIGS; promoter comparison; dual-LUC; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; temperature-responsive promoter assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development stage; promoter insertion with low-temperature-responsive element; 25°C promoted expression and AsA accumulation more than hotter conditions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) for single-gene overexpression; native promoter in one multigene design
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a major control point/key gene in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target; promoter variation and coding sequence are useful for boosting AsA, especially in multigene stacks
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/annotation: NGDC GWHETKS00000000.1, PRJCA027126; RNA-seq: PRJCA027090
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A 545-bp promoter insertion unique to R. roxburghii introduced a WRKY-binding W-box and an LTR element, explaining species-specific high AsA accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Rosa roxburghii; Lactuca sativa; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Guinong 5; Italian lettuce (L. sativa L. var. ramosa Hort.); Col-0
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dehydroascorbate back to ascorbic acid using reducing equivalents from glutathione-dependent systems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox buffer in plants; beneficial nutrient for human health
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit in chestnut rose; leaves in transgenic lettuce and Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression pattern correlated with high AsA accumulation in R. roxburghii and showed same directional pattern as key D-galacturonic acid genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in R. roxburghii fruit under 25°C condition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes AsA accumulation through regeneration of reduced AsA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RroxDHAR2 was identified as a candidate AsA accumulation gene in chestnut rose. 2. Overexpression in lettuce increased leaf AsA by about 0.8- to 2.7-fold, and overexpression in Arabidopsis also increased AsA. 3. RroxTTG2 binds the RroxDHAR2 promoter and activates its expression. 4. Inclusion in multigene stacks contributed to the strongest AsA enhancement in lettuce.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and qPCR correlation analysis; heterologous overexpression in lettuce and Arabidopsis; dual-LUC; yeast one-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development stage; 25°C treatment enhanced expression and AsA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) for single-gene overexpression; native promoter in one multigene design
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful partner in gene stacking with D-galacturonic acid pathway genes and TTG2 to maximize AsA improvement
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/annotation: NGDC GWHETKS00000000.1, PRJCA027126; RNA-seq: PRJCA027090
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes synergistic participation of D-galacturonic acid pathway genes and DHAR-mediated recycling in high AsA accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Rosa roxburghii; Lactuca sativa; Arabidopsis thaliana
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Guinong 5; Italian lettuce (L. sativa L. var. ramosa Hort.); Col-0
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: AsA biosynthesis and AsA regeneration through activation of multiple pathway genes; also trichome development
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates RroxGalUR, RroxPME, and RroxDHAR2 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via promoter binding to W-box-containing cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental stage/spatiotemporal regulation; upstream activation by RroxGL3 and RroxEGL3 during trichome-development module
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: RroxGL3 and RroxEGL3 bind the RroxTTG2 promoter and activate its expression; linked to MBW trichome regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator enabling high AsA accumulation in chestnut rose by coordinately activating multiple AsA-related genes; broader and stronger control than citrus TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox buffer in plants; beneficial nutrient for human health
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit in chestnut rose; leaves in transgenic lettuce and Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High expression at early bud stage for trichome development and during fruit development strongly correlated with AsA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In chestnut rose fruit, expression is significantly correlated with AsA during S1-S4 but not during early bud trichome stages; heterologous expression increases AsA in Arabidopsis and lettuce
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes AsA accumulation and trichome formation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RroxTTG2 was identified among TFs positively correlated with AsA accumulation. 2. It binds the inserted W-box-containing RroxGalUR promoter and also activates RroxPME and RroxDHAR2. 3. VIGS of RroxTTG2 in chestnut rose fruit reduced AsA by 14%. 4. Overexpression in Arabidopsis increased leaf AsA by 38%–80% and increased trichome number; complementation rescued the attg2 mutant. 5. In multigene lettuce transformants, inclusion of RroxTTG2 contributed to the strongest AsA increase.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq correlation; qPCR; dual-LUC; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; VIGS in fruit; heterologous overexpression and complementation in Arabidopsis; multigene transformation in lettuce
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; temperature influences downstream target expression and AsA accumulation, but RroxTTG2 itself was reported not to change in response to temperature fluctuations
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) in overexpression and multigene constructs; AtUBQ10 used in 4TU construct framework
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent regulatory engineering target for multigene AsA biofortification because it coordinately activates multiple AsA genes and can be stacked with pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Also alters trichome density and enhances anthocyanin accumulation, indicating pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/annotation: NGDC GWHETKS00000000.1, PRJCA027126; RNA-seq: PRJCA027090
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with sweet orange CsTTG2, RroxTTG2 has broader downstream control, which likely contributes to the much higher AsA level in chestnut rose.
